channel.title: RoundSignal - Weekly funded-startup signals
channel.description: Each week: freshly-funded startups, the role to contact, and a ready-to-send angle.

item.title: RoundSignal 2026-W27: 21 freshly-funded accounts to sell to this week
item.description:
RoundSignal Issue 2026-W27

Freshly-funded B2B SaaS & tech startups (pre-seed -> Series B)

21 trigger-ready accounts, scored and contextualized.

Wultra

heat 88

Trigger: €6.8M Series A, led by Seventure Partners (EU-Startups, as of 29 Jun 2026)

Why now: Fresh Series A cybersecurity/identity startup scaling its platform and racing to ship digital identity wallet capabilities — they will be hiring and building fast over the next quarter.

Who to contact: VP Engineering / Head of Product

Angle: Reference their EU Digital Identity Wallet roadmap and offer post-quantum-aware QA or a dedicated dev pod to hit the eIDAS rollout timeline.

source

Almetra (formerly Deltia)

heat 86

Trigger: €16.3M Series A, led by blisce/ with NAP, Merantix Capital, Robert Bosch (Tech.eu, as of 25 Jun 2026)

Why now: Just rebranded from Deltia AND raised a solid Series A — the rebrand plus new capital means demand for new positioning, web, and go-to-market support is immediate.

Who to contact: CMO / Head of Marketing

Angle: Congratulate them on the Deltia→Almetra rebrand and offer a fast brand-launch sprint (messaging + site) so the new name lands before competitors notice.

source

Serpier

heat 82

Trigger: €1.4M round from True Collective and EIFO (Tech.eu, as of 25 Jun 2026)

Why now: A Danish martech startup with new capital to expand its AI marketing platform for e-commerce — directly in your wheelhouse for growth/RevOps partnerships and prime for hiring.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO or Head of Growth

Angle: Open with a teardown of one e-commerce customer's search visibility and show how your growth team complements their platform rollout.

source

Kalipso

heat 80

Trigger: $3.2M funding round (Tech.eu, as of 25 Jun 2026)

Why now: Barcelona regtech with fresh capital to scale a compliance platform — early-stage and lawyer/engineer-built means they'll need design, dev and content help to commercialize fast.

Who to contact: Co-founder / Head of Product

Angle: Reference the complexity of operationalizing regulatory change and offer UX/product-design support to make their compliance workflows enterprise-ready.

source

Fika Jobs

heat 78

Trigger: $4M round to build a video-first AI hiring platform (TechCrunch, as of 23 Jun 2026)

Why now: Fresh $4M and a clear scaling agenda — a recruiting-tech startup that itself will be hiring and needs growth/marketing to land early customers.

Who to contact: CEO / Head of Growth

Angle: Pitch a launch-marketing or demand-gen sprint targeting talent teams, leveraging their AI-interview angle as a strong PR hook.

source

Vegvisir

heat 72

Trigger: Venture investment from Iron Wolf Capital; amount not disclosed (Tech.eu, as of 29 Jun 2026)

Why now: Estonian defence-tech startup with fresh capital for its command-and-control software layer — building a software product means engineering and design needs ramp quickly.

Who to contact: CTO / Head of Engineering

Angle: Offer specialized software/UX support for mission-critical C2 interfaces; note that amount undisclosed so position as a flexible early-stage partner.

source

AethexAI

heat 70

Trigger: $3M pre-seed round (afrotech.com, as of 22 Jun 2026)

Why now: Full-stack voice AI infrastructure startup with new pre-seed cash targeting emerging markets — early-stage and in need of go-to-market, dev and brand help.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: Reference their emerging-markets enterprise focus and offer localized GTM or developer-relations support to land first enterprise logos.

source

Ground

heat 70

Trigger: $3.6M pre-seed (Superstate co-founder) to help fintechs access onchain yield (The Block / Crypto Briefing, corroborated, as of 24 Jun 2026)

Why now: Two-source-corroborated pre-seed fintech infra startup — early-stage, building product fast and needs dev, design and content to reach fintech customers.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: Note the dual coverage on their launch and offer technical content/developer marketing to build trust with fintech buyers of onchain yield rails.

source

SE3 Labs

heat 68

Trigger: Emerges from stealth, backed by Lakestar, Seedcamp and EWOR; amount not disclosed (Tech.eu, as of 26 Jun 2026)

Why now: Spatial AI company just out of stealth with notable backers — stealth exits are prime moments for PR, brand and first-hire support.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO or Head of Marketing

Angle: Time outreach to their stealth-exit moment: offer a launch PR push and positioning to capitalize on the Lakestar/Seedcamp credibility.

source

Neurometric

heat 62

Trigger: $4M pre-seed (The SaaS News, as of 26 Jun 2026)

Why now: (unverified) Single thin source. Fresh pre-seed SaaS startup with capital to build team and product — strong early-stage services fit if verified.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: Reach out referencing their pre-seed milestone and offer a first-90-days product/design package to accelerate MVP-to-market.

source

Kyrok

heat 62

Trigger: €3.1M pre-seed (FinSMEs + The SaaS News, corroborated, as of 23 Jun 2026)

Why now: Two-source-corroborated pre-seed raise — early-stage and building out team and GTM, a good window for services partnerships.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: Mention the multiple writeups of their €3.1M and offer a focused go-to-market or brand sprint to convert buzz into pipeline.

source

Daya

heat 60

Trigger: $2.4M pre-seed for stablecoin payment rails for African businesses (The SaaS News + WeeTracker, corroborated, as of 24-25 Jun 2026)

Why now: Two-source-corroborated pre-seed fintech — early-stage with new capital to build out rails and team across African markets.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: Reference their African stablecoin rails focus and offer localized growth/RevOps or dev support to scale merchant onboarding.

source

Clairva

heat 55

Trigger: $500K led by Venture Catalysts (Entrackr, as of 29 Jun 2026)

Why now: (unverified) Single source. AI data infrastructure startup with fresh capital — very early stage, fits for dev and design partners willing to grow with them.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: Open with the AI data-infra angle and offer a lean dev/design retainer suited to a $500K early budget.

source

Lytmus AI

heat 54

Trigger: ₹5 crore (~$600K) pre-seed led by Boundless Ventures (Business Standard + Entrackr, corroborated, as of 28-29 Jun 2026)

Why now: Two-source-corroborated pre-seed AI startup — fresh and small, fitting for early dev/design partners willing to scale alongside.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: Cite the Boundless Ventures backing and offer a cost-efficient product/design engagement scaled to a pre-seed budget.

source

Archimede

heat 54

Trigger: €1.5M Seed, led by Primo Capital SGR (EU-Startups, as of 29 Jun 2026)

Why now: Italian DeepTech that explicitly plans to strengthen technical and commercial teams and run enterprise pilots — clear near-term hiring and GTM needs.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO or Head of Commercial

Angle: Reference their stated enterprise-pilot push and offer commercial/marketing support to convert pilots into paying contracts.

source

Lumenai

heat 48

Trigger: Pre-seed round, amount not disclosed (The SaaS News, as of 24 Jun 2026)

Why now: (unverified) Single thin source with no amount. SaaS startup at pre-seed — speculative early-stage opportunity for services partners.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: Reach out on their pre-seed news and offer a discovery session to scope MVP product/design needs (amount undisclosed, so keep it flexible).

source

Wayout International

heat 46

Trigger: €2.42M Series A extension (Tech.eu, as of 25 Jun 2026)

Why now: Swedish water-infra developer with a fresh Series A extension to scale — they'll be building out commercial and ops capacity, fitting for RevOps/marketing partners.

Who to contact: Head of Commercial / COO

Angle: Offer go-to-market support to package their distributed water infrastructure for municipal and enterprise buyers as they scale post-extension.

source

Intu Diagnostics

heat 42

Trigger: €1.1M strategic bridge round (BSV Ventures, SAB, angels); total >€3M (EU-Startups, as of 26 Jun 2026)

Why now: German diagnostics startup with a fresh bridge to strengthen IP and product — early-stage and possibly in need of branding and commercial prep, though healthtech is adjacent to core vertical.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: Note the bridge round and offer support readying their lab-free platform story for commercial/regulatory stakeholders.

source

Subatron

heat 35

Trigger: €162K (CHF 150K) from Venture Kick (Tech.eu, as of 29 Jun 2026)

Why now: Very early Swiss engineering startup with a small grant-style round — limited budget but a fresh trigger for low-cost early dev/design help.

Who to contact: Founder/CEO

Angle: Congratulate on the Venture Kick award and offer a lean, milestone-based engagement suited to their small early budget.

source

Openchip

heat 34

Trigger: €115M SETT investment for AI/HPC chip design (EU-Startups, as of 29 Jun 2026)

Why now: Large semiconductor scale-up with major government capital — likely scaling teams and operations, though hardware focus is partly off-vertical for B2B SaaS sellers.

Who to contact: VP People / Head of Marketing

Angle: Offer recruiting or employer-branding support to help them hire chip and software talent quickly against their €115M expansion mandate.

source

Gaussion

heat 32

Trigger: €24.5M ($28M) round co-led by BGF and AlbionVC; total funding >€38M (EU-Startups, as of 29 Jun 2026)

Why now: London DeepTech energy-intelligence company with a sizeable raise — scaling and hiring, though battery/hardware focus is adjacent to the core SaaS vertical.

Who to contact: Head of People / Marketing

Angle: Position recruiting or PR support to capitalize on their funding momentum across EV, drone and data-center markets.

source

© RoundSignal. Signals sourced from public news; verify before outreach.
